skill.name: lark-workflow-meeting-summary
skill.description: Summarizes meeting notes from Lark within a specified time range and generates structured reports.
skill.summary: Aggregates meeting summaries from Lark and produces organized reports for weekly reviews or ad-hoc queries.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 41 patterns (external commands, filesystem operations) but all are false positives. The skill uses lark-cli commands legitimately as a CLI wrapper for Lark's meeting APIs. Path references like '../lark-shared/SKILL.md' are documentation links to other skill modules, not path traversal attacks. No malicious patterns detected after semantic review.

content.user_title: Summarize and report Lark meeting notes
content.value_statement: Manually tracking and summarizing meetings across multiple days is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automatically queries Lark meetings within any time range and generates structured summary reports.
actual_capabilities: Query Lark meetings within custom date ranges (day, week, month)
Retrieve meeting notes and verbatim transcripts via lark-cli
Generate structured summary reports with meeting statistics
Create or update Lark cloud documents with compiled summaries
Handle pagination for large meeting lists (up to 30 per page)
Batch process up to 50 meeting notes and 10 documents per request
limitations: Requires user to authenticate with lark-cli before use (vc and drive domains)
Only supports user-level authentication, not bot or service accounts
Maximum search range is one month per query (longer ranges require multiple calls)
Some meetings may not have notes available and will be marked accordingly

use_cases.title: Weekly meeting summary for team leads
use_cases.description: Generate a weekly report of all meetings attended by the team, including links to notes and transcripts for easy reference.

use_cases.title: End-of-day meeting recap
use_cases.description: Quickly review all meetings from the current day with summaries and action items extracted from meeting notes.

use_cases.title: Monthly meeting audit
use_cases.description: Compile all meetings from the past month for compliance or time-tracking purposes with full documentation links.

prompt_templates.title: Basic daily summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize all my meetings from today in Lark.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick end-of-day review of meetings attended

prompt_templates.title: Weekly report with statistics
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a weekly meeting summary report for this week including total meeting count and links to all notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Weekly team sync preparation or personal time tracking

prompt_templates.title: Custom date range query
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve all meetings and their notes from March 1st to March 15th and compile them into a single summary document.
prompt_templates.scenario: Project retrospective or quarterly review preparation

prompt_templates.title: Meeting notes export
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all meetings from the past week that have available notes, extract the key discussion points, and create a new Lark document with the compiled summary.

output_examples.input: Summarize my meetings from this week
output: Weekly Meeting Summary (Mon Jan 15 - Fri Jan 19, 2026)
Total Meetings: 12 | With Notes: 9 | Without Notes: 3

output_examples.input: What meetings did I have yesterday?

best_practices: Always specify a clear time range in your request (today, this week, last month) for accurate results
For large time ranges spanning multiple months, expect the skill to make multiple batched API calls
Review generated summaries before sharing as some meetings may lack detailed notes
anti_patterns: Do not query overly broad date ranges (e.g., 'all meetings from last year') without a specific purpose
Avoid running during peak hours if querying large datasets as it may impact API rate limits
Do not share meeting summary documents externally without checking note visibility permissions

faq.question: What authentication is required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need to authenticate with lark-cli using 'lark-cli auth login --domain vc' for basic meeting queries. Add 'drive' domain if you want to create summary documents.

faq.question: What happens if a meeting has no notes?
faq.answer: Meetings without notes are marked as 'No notes available' in the summary report. This occurs when notes were not created or you lack permission to view them.

faq.question: Can I customize the output format of the summary?
faq.answer: Yes, you can request specific formats like 'bullet points only', 'table format', or 'include action items' when asking for the summary.

faq.question: How far back can I query meetings?
faq.answer: You can query any historical meeting accessible in your Lark workspace. However, queries spanning more than one month are automatically split into multiple API calls.

faq.question: Can this skill create a shareable document with the summary?
faq.answer: Yes, you can ask the skill to create a new Lark document or append to an existing one with the compiled meeting summary.

faq.question: Does this skill work with recurring meetings?
faq.answer: Yes, each occurrence of a recurring meeting is treated as a separate meeting entry and will appear individually in the summary if it falls within your date range.
